nativeRange.habitat: Steep seaward reefs over sand patches, rubble, and hard substrate, generally at depths of 25–90 m

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding primarily on zooplankton and small crustaceans suspended in the water column. Aquarium specimens have been known to accept frozen mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, finely chopped seafood, and high-quality marine pellets. Feeding 2–3 times daily in small portions is generally advised.

summary: Striking deepwater dartfish with a vivid yellow-orange face fading to lavender and white. Generally considered reef-safe, peaceful, and a good candidate for mixed reef tanks; tends to be shy and is a known jumper requiring a covered aquarium.
careNotes:
The Helfrichi Firefish is generally considered one of the more visually impressive dartfish, displaying a vivid yellow-orange head that transitions to a lavender-purple body. It tends to inhabit the lower portion of the tank, often retreating to crevices or burrows among live rock and rubble when startled.

A secure, tight-fitting lid or screen cover is strongly advised, as this species has been known to jump from open aquaria.

Conspecific aggression: two individuals will generally only coexist peacefully if they are a bonded mated pair. Single specimens or unpaired individuals have been known to fight aggressively with others of the same species.

Diet enrichment is generally recommended; color loss over time has been reported in aquarium specimens fed unenriched foods. Varied frozen foods including mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, and cyclops tend to maintain coloration.

This species is commonly found in deeper reef waters in the wild (25–90 m), which may explain its preference for shaded hiding spots and its reported sensitivity to bright, direct lighting in some aquariums.

Adult size disagreement across sources: FishBase and ReefApp report a maximum of 6 cm TL; Reeflings.com reports up to 9 cm. Values averaged to give a range of 4–7.5 cm. Temperature range averaged across FishBase habitat modeling (25.7–29°C), Reeflings (22–26°C), and hobbyist guidance (22–25.6°C) to produce 23–27°C. Minimum tank volume averaged across sources: Reeflings (75 L), ReefApp (100 L), and general hobbyist guidance (75 L) → ~83 L, rounded to 80 L.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to form monogamous pairs in the wild. Captive breeding has been documented only rarely; larvae require specialized plankton-based feeding regimes and are not routinely raised in home aquaria. Captive pairs have been observed spawning but rearing success rates remain very low.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: LiveAquaria individual product page for Helfrichi Firefish returned 404; collection page confirmed species listing and general dartfish care notes. Specific husbandry values sourced from cross-verified secondary sources.

additional.name: Reeflings
additional.notes: Provided size (9 cm), temperature (22–26°C), salinity (SG 1.020–1.025), pH (8.1–8.4), min tank (75 L), reef safe, diet, and native range. Adult size higher than FishBase; values averaged.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed max adult size 6.0 cm TL, family Microdesmidae, order Gobiiformes, temperature modeled at 25.7–29°C, native range SE Pacific (French Polynesia) and wider Central/Western Pacific, planktivore diet classification.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Adult size 6 cm, min tank 100 L, salinity ~SG 1.026. Cross-confirmed reef-safe, shy and peaceful temperament, Microdesmidae family.

fish.breedingNotes: Captive spawning has been observed in bonded pairs but larval rearing is extremely difficult and not routinely achieved in home aquaria.
